name: Dynatrace MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Dynatrace MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Dynatrace's AI-powered observability platform — querying metrics, problems, and events with DQL (Dynatrace Query Language), managing alerts and notifications, analyzing service topology, and integrating Dynatrace's Davis AI insights into agent-driven SRE and AIOps workflows.
use_cases: Querying application performance metrics using DQL from SRE agents
Analyzing Dynatrace Davis AI problems and root cause analysis from incident agents
Monitoring service topology and dependencies from reliability agents
Managing alerting and notification policies from ops agents
Correlating distributed traces with infrastructure metrics from debugging agents
Generating SLO compliance reports from reporting agents
not_for: Teams using Datadog, New Relic, or AppDynamics as primary APM platform
Simple log-only use cases (Dynatrace is full-stack observability)
best_when: An agent needs to query or act on Dynatrace observability data — especially leveraging Davis AI for automated root cause analysis.
avoid_when: You're using Datadog, New Relic, or another APM platform.

auth.notes: Dynatrace API tokens with specific permission scopes. OAuth 2.0 with client credentials for automation. Tokens are environment-specific. Granular permission model with 50+ scope options.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free trial: 15 days full platform access
pricing.paid_tiers: Full-stack monitoring: ~$0.08/hour per 8GB host; DPS-based pricing available
pricing.notes: Dynatrace Platform Subscription (DPS) is the modern pricing model. Host-based pricing also available. Enterprise platform with significant per-host cost. MCP server is official open source.

performance.rate_limits: 500 req/min for most API endpoints; DQL queries have separate limits

known_agent_gotchas: DQL (Dynatrace Query Language) is proprietary — different from PromQL or SPL
Environment ID embedded in all API URLs — must use correct environment URL
API token must include specific scopes for each API type — minimal permission setup required
Managed Dynatrace vs Dynatrace SaaS have different base URL patterns
Time series queries return Dynatrace metric key format — schema varies by metric
Davis AI problems are analyzed automatically — agents should query problems, not re-analyze
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Granular API token scopes (50+ permissions). OAuth 2.0 with client credentials. SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR, FedRAMP. Official Dynatrace OSS MCP server.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Highly granular API token scopes. OAuth 2.0. SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR, FedRAMP. Official Dynatrace OSS MCP server.
